title: The One Where Ross Finds Out

jokes.text: What is it about me? Do I not look fun enough? Is there something repellent about me?
jokes.setup: Character desperately questioning their attractiveness
jokes.punchline: The extreme self-doubt and word choice 'repellent' - treating herself like a bug spray
jokes.explanation: Solid character-driven setup establishing Rachel's insecurity with a mildly amusing word choice

jokes.text: A woman literally passed through me
jokes.setup: Question about how the party went
jokes.punchline: Impossible physical description of being ignored - becoming literally invisible
jokes.explanation: Clever exaggeration with great economy - efficiently conveys total social invisibility

jokes.text: I've put on a little weight?
jokes.setup: Someone trying to be supportive mentions weight gain
jokes.punchline: Rachel's horrified realization that she didn't know she'd gained weight
jokes.explanation: Classic Friends-style misunderstanding with good character specificity

jokes.text: No, not weight. More like insulation
jokes.setup: Trying to backtrack from the weight comment
jokes.punchline: Making it worse by comparing her to a house with better insulation
jokes.explanation: Clever attempt to soften the blow that actually makes it funnier

jokes.text: that might get in the way of my lying-around time
jokes.setup: Monica offering to help Chandler work out
jokes.punchline: Chandler's commitment to laziness as if it's a scheduled activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler line - treats laziness with mock importance, very character-specific

jokes.text: if we put on spandex and my boobs are bigger than yours, I'm going home
jokes.setup: Agreeing to work out with Monica
jokes.punchline: Chandler's self-aware concern about his physique in workout clothes
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor with body image comedy that fits Chandler

jokes.text: Ooh, can't make hands meet!
jokes.setup: Rachel hugging Chandler to reassure him
jokes.punchline: She can't get her arms around him, confirming the weight issue
jokes.explanation: Visual gag that pays off the weight setup - competent but predictable

jokes.text: Just never seen you in little stretchy pants before
jokes.setup: Monica noticing Chandler in workout clothes
jokes.punchline: Her awkward description of spandex as 'little stretchy pants'
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor with Monica's characteristic awkwardness

jokes.text: And we're changing
jokes.setup: Monica's comment about his stretchy pants
jokes.punchline: Chandler immediately wanting to change out of them
jokes.explanation: Quick, efficient reaction that shows Chandler's self-consciousness

jokes.text: Five more and I'll flash you
jokes.setup: Monica trying to motivate Chandler to do more exercises
jokes.punchline: Offering an inappropriate incentive that makes everyone uncomfortable
jokes.explanation: Monica's overzealous trainer personality leading to awkward territory

jokes.text: One... two... two and a half
jokes.setup: Monica's flash offer
jokes.punchline: Chandler suddenly finding motivation but still struggling with the count
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Chandler's sudden motivation but continued weakness

jokes.text: Just show me one of them
jokes.setup: The flash negotiation continuing
jokes.punchline: Chandler negotiating down to a partial flash
jokes.explanation: Continues the escalation with Chandler's characteristic negotiation style

jokes.text: Ow. Ow. Ow. Ow. Ow. Ow. Ow. Ow. Ow. Ow.
jokes.setup: Chandler doing the workout
jokes.punchline: Extended sequence of pain showing how out of shape he is
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy but repetitive and not particularly clever

jokes.text: She's got me doing butt-clenches at my desk. And now they won't bring me my mail anymore
jokes.setup: Chandler complaining about Monica's workout regime
jokes.punchline: The logical consequence of doing butt exercises at work
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with a perfect logical consequence - very quotable

jokes.text: The guy still won't put out? Nope. Zilch. Nothing. Uh-uh.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Phoebe's dating situation
jokes.punchline: Using typically male language about female sexual availability
jokes.explanation: Good role reversal humor with Joey and Phoebe's casual attitudes

jokes.text: Maybe he drives his car on the other side of the road. If you know what I mean. No. What do you mean? He's not British.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to delicately suggest the guy might be gay
jokes.punchline: Phoebe completely missing the euphemism and taking it literally
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with excellent character work - Phoebe's literal-mindedness vs Joey's euphemisms

jokes.text: No, I felt it on my hip. I could tell.
jokes.setup: Discussion about whether you can tell someone's sexuality from a look
jokes.punchline: Phoebe clarifying she felt his physical arousal, not just emotional connection
jokes.explanation: Great cringe moment with perfect Phoebe oversharing and misdirection payoff

jokes.text: Joey, be a pal. Lift up my hand and smack her with it
jokes.setup: Chandler is too tired to move from Monica's workouts
jokes.punchline: Asking Joey to use his own hand to hit Monica because he's too weak
jokes.explanation: Creative physical comedy setup showing Chandler's exhaustion in an absurd way

jokes.text: He can press her up against that window as much as he wants. For all I care, he can throw her through the damn thing.
jokes.setup: Rachel claiming she's over Ross and his new relationship
jokes.punchline: Her 'casual' attitude escalating to violence, revealing she's not over it
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Rachel's true feelings beneath her fake casual attitude

jokes.text: Can I come over tomorrow and pick up Fluffy Meowington's cat toys?
jokes.setup: Monica making Ross say the cat's full name
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous formal name 'Fluffy Meowington' that Ross has to say with dignity
jokes.explanation: Silly name humor with good setup - moderately quotable

jokes.text: That's something the two of you will be able to enjoy for a really, really, really long time
jokes.setup: Ross and Julie getting a cat together
jokes.punchline: Rachel's fake enthusiasm with the repetitive 'really' showing her true feelings
jokes.explanation: Good passive-aggressive delivery showing Rachel's barely contained jealousy

jokes.text: With a man. Ahem, okay. You guys have a really, uh, good night and you two have a, uh, really good cat. Heh.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to leave gracefully after the cat discussion
jokes.punchline: Her awkward overcompensation and weird cat blessing
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe moment showing Rachel's discomfort through awkward over-pleasantries

jokes.text: We're not supposed to take these when we leave
jokes.setup: Rachel leaving the coffee shop
jokes.punchline: She's apparently stealing sugar packets or condiments in her emotional state
jokes.explanation: Small visual gag showing Rachel's scattered emotional state through petty theft

jokes.text: How long do cats live? Figuring you don't throw them under a bus or something?
jokes.setup: Rachel asking about cat lifespans on her date
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of violence against cats as an exception
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor showing Rachel's emotional state bleeding into casual conversation

jokes.text: Monica told you I was cuter than this, didn't she?
jokes.setup: Michael noticing Rachel is distracted and not enjoying the date
jokes.punchline: His assumption that he's the problem, not her emotional baggage
jokes.explanation: Sweet self-deprecating humor from the date who doesn't understand the real situation

jokes.text: Yeah, he wishes
jokes.setup: Michael asking if Ross is an old boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Rachel's bitter response suggesting Ross wanted more than friendship
jokes.explanation: Efficient bitter humor with good economy - reveals Rachel's perspective on their history

jokes.text: So... Did you ever get a pet with a girlfriend?
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to redirect conversation to Michael
jokes.punchline: Immediately bringing it back to the exact same topic she's obsessing over
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Rachel's complete inability to let go of the cat issue

jokes.text: I said, 'Excellent butt, great rack.' Really? That's so sweet. I mean, I'm officially offended. But that's so sweet.
jokes.setup: Joey revealing his first impression of Phoebe to boost her confidence
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's conflicted reaction - simultaneously offended and flattered
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe response - her unique logic of being simultaneously offended and pleased

jokes.text: Ugh, you are so yum!
jokes.setup: End of conversation about getting advice
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's weird way of expressing gratitude by calling someone food
jokes.explanation: Pure Phoebe weirdness - using food terminology for people in her unique way

jokes.text: Why can't they get one of those bugs? One of those fruit flies. Those things that live for a day. What are they called? What are they called? Fruit flies? Yes! Thank you.
jokes.setup: Rachel obsessing over the cat's lifespan
jokes.punchline: Her manic suggestion of insects and circular logic about fruit flies
jokes.explanation: Shows Rachel's mental spiral through repetitive, circular thinking - good character work but a bit drawn out

jokes.text: But only because I've been playing the movie Diner in my head
jokes.setup: Michael asking if she's having fun
jokes.punchline: Rachel's oddly specific mental escape mechanism during the bad date
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and absurd way to describe mental disengagement from a bad date

jokes.text: God, you're brilliant! Why didn't I think of that? How do I get that?
jokes.setup: Michael mentioning closure
jokes.punchline: Rachel's sudden manic excitement about this revelation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Rachel's emotional instability and tendency to latch onto simple solutions

jokes.text: All right. Fine. I'll call you back. Thank you.
jokes.setup: Rachel desperately needing to borrow a stranger's phone
jokes.punchline: The person's exasperated capitulation to Rachel's manic insistence
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe comedy showing how Rachel's emotional state affects innocent bystanders

jokes.text: Who, by the way, I think you should name Michael. And you see there, I'm thinking of names so obviously I am over you.
jokes.setup: Rachel leaving a message claiming to be over Ross
jokes.punchline: Her twisted logic that naming suggestions prove she's moved on
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Rachel's desperate self-deception through illogical reasoning

jokes.text: Because it's Sunday! It's God's day. Ha, hey. If you say stop, then we stop. Stop. No, come on! We can't stop!
jokes.setup: Chandler not wanting to work out on Sunday
jokes.punchline: Monica immediately contradicting her own promise when he takes her up on it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica moment - her competitive obsessiveness overriding her promises

jokes.text: I am the energy train and you are on board! Whoo.
jokes.setup: Monica's motivational workout speech
jokes.punchline: Her ridiculous train metaphor delivered with complete seriousness
jokes.explanation: Classic Monica over-the-top enthusiasm with a silly but memorable metaphor

jokes.text: Um, I think there was a restaurant. I know there was wine.
jokes.setup: Monica asking how Rachel's date went
jokes.punchline: Rachel's vague recollection showing how distracted she was
jokes.explanation: Good understatement showing Rachel's complete mental absence from her date

jokes.text: I feel like I had a dream about you last night but I don't remember
jokes.setup: Rachel sensing something about Ross but can't place it
jokes.punchline: The audience knows about the message but Rachel's blocked it out
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony building tension toward the message reveal

jokes.text: Rach, I got a message from you! Who's Michael? Oh, my God. God, Ross, no! Hang up the phone. Give me the phone! Give me the phone! Give me the phone! Give me the...
jokes.setup: Ross discovering Rachel's drunken message
jokes.punchline: Rachel's manic desperation to stop him from hearing more
jokes.explanation: Good panic comedy sequence with escalating desperation, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: When were you... under me?
jokes.setup: Rachel saying she's 'over' Ross
jokes.punchline: Ross misunderstanding the phrase and creating accidental sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Solid double entendre arising from Ross's confusion about the idiom

jokes.text: You had feelings for me first. Whoa, huh. You know about my...? You know I had...? You know?
jokes.setup: Ross revealing he knows about Rachel's feelings
jokes.punchline: Rachel's stammering realization that her secret is out
jokes.explanation: Good revelation moment with realistic stammering dialogue

jokes.text: I'm gonna stand... and I'm gonna walk. I'm walking and I am standing.
jokes.setup: Rachel processing the overwhelming revelation
jokes.punchline: Her need to narrate basic physical actions while having a breakdown
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing Rachel's mental state through basic action narration

jokes.text: I've got a cab. I've got a girlfriend. I'm gonna go get a cat. Okay. Okay. Cat!
jokes.setup: Ross trying to process the situation
jokes.punchline: His attempt at normalcy ending with an emphatic, almost aggressive 'Cat!'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Ross's mental breakdown manifesting as cat obsession

jokes.text: I can't find the remote
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking why he's watching a rabbi with electric guitar
jokes.punchline: Joey's matter-of-fact explanation for watching bizarre television
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and deadpan delivery - classic Joey logic

jokes.text: Is he gonna call me the next day? And Where is this going? So he said he wanted to hold off until he was prepared to be serious.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining Scott's reasoning about emotional attachment to sex
jokes.punchline: The guy being more emotionally concerned about casual sex than Phoebe
jokes.explanation: Good role reversal with traditional gender expectations flipped

jokes.text: Let me get this straight. He got you to beg to sleep with him. He got you to say he never has to call you again. And he got you thinking this is a great idea? This man is my god.
jokes.setup: Joey processing Phoebe's story about Scott
jokes.punchline: Joey's awed recognition of Scott's masterful manipulation
jokes.explanation: Excellent revelation comedy with perfect Joey perspective - admiring the technique rather than condemning the manipulation

jokes.text: I didn't get a cat. Oh, that's, um, interesting. No. No, it's not interesting. Okay? It's very, very not interesting. It's actually a hundred percent the opposite of interesting.
jokes.setup: Monica's polite response to Ross not getting a cat
jokes.punchline: Ross's increasingly manic insistence that it's not interesting at all
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Ross's emotional state through over-correction

jokes.text: Not... Not... Not... every night
jokes.setup: Rachel pointing out they hung out every night for a year
jokes.punchline: Ross's pathetic attempt to deny the obvious truth
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross caught in obvious lie

jokes.text: There was one Italian guy, okay? And do you have a point?
jokes.setup: Ross bringing up her Italian guys twice
jokes.punchline: Rachel's irritated correction and demand for relevance
jokes.explanation: Good defensive response with Rachel's characteristic directness

jokes.text: I don't need your stupid ship! Good! Good! And you know what? Now I got closure!
jokes.setup: Ross saying 'this ship has sailed' about their relationship
jokes.punchline: Rachel rejecting his ship metaphor and claiming closure from the fight
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the closure concept with angry escalation

jokes.text: What? Heh. Nothing. Except tell you, uh, it's wonderful how much energy you have. Well, thanks. Especially considering how tough it's been for you to find work.
jokes.setup: Monica calling Chandler 'fat boy' and asking what he'll do
jokes.punchline: Chandler's fake compliment that's actually psychological warfare
jokes.explanation: Excellent passive-aggressive comedy - Chandler's subtle psychological attack disguised as support

jokes.text: You can't tell your parents you were fired, because they'd be disappointed. It's not as if you have a boyfriend's shoulder to cry on.
jokes.setup: Continuing his fake supportive monologue
jokes.punchline: Systematically attacking all of Monica's insecurities
jokes.explanation: Masterful escalation of psychological manipulation disguised as encouragement

jokes.text: If it were me, I'd have difficulty getting out of bed at all. You know, I try to stay positive. So you? You feel like going for a run?
jokes.setup: Chandler's devastating psychological attack
jokes.punchline: Monica completely defeated, agreeing to skip the run
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the psychological warfare sequence - Chandler's total victory through emotional manipulation

tmdb_overview: A drunk Rachel confesses feelings to Ross on his answering machine. Monica becomes Chandler's trainer; Phoebe struggles with her boyfriend.
